mnemonics: FOIL: First Order Inductive Learner. Like a FOIL wrapper, it learns a concept by wrapping rules around examples.
keyPoints: FOIL is a top-down, greedy algorithm for learning first-order Horn clauses.
It uses a sequential covering algorithm to learn one rule at a time, removing the positive examples covered by each new rule.
It employs a general-to-specific search, starting with an empty rule and specializing it by adding literals to the body.
commonMistakes: Confusing FOIL (the algorithm) with the FOIL gain heuristic it uses to select literals.
Assuming it learns propositional rules instead of first-order rules with variables.
Forgetting that it handles noisy data by learning rules that do not necessarily cover all positive examples.
